_meta.description: Canonical financial structure — fund accounting, expense ratios, audit trail. Does NOT contain actual transaction-level data; that lives in the accounting system. This file describes the chart-of-accounts, ratio targets, and reporting cadence.

githubSponsors.note: personal account only; org account requires separate enrollment

programServices._purpose: Direct mission delivery — should be >= 75% of total expenses for high-rated nonprofits

fundraising._purpose: Cost of raising funds — keep under 10% of total

expenseRatioTargets.rationale: Charity Navigator 4-star bands; BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards align

unrestricted.policy: Default category; only re-classify with explicit donor restriction

permanentlyRestricted.policy: Not solicited until reserve policy met

audit.trigger: annual revenue >= $250,000 OR per state requirement (CA, NY, etc.)

auditCommittee.note: Required when external audit begins; chair must be independent of management

reportingCadence.publicAnnualReport: annual, published within 6 months of fiscal year end

founderCompensation.rationale: Founder is unpaid until reserve policy met AND board adopts compensation policy

donorAcknowledgments.deadlineToProvide: by January 31 of year following gift
irsLanguageRequired: Amount of cash contribution OR description of non-cash
Statement: "No goods or services were provided in exchange for this contribution" (when true)
Description and good-faith estimate of value of any goods/services (when applicable)

transparencyCommitments: All board members listed publicly on /governance/
Form 990 published within 30 days of filing
Annual report includes: revenue sources, expense breakdown, programs delivered, board roster, key staff
No paid endorsements; no quid-pro-quo donor recognition that constitutes private benefit
Salary range published (when staff hired); top 5 earners named on Form 990 Schedule J
